subject: Databases
title: Window Functions
description: A micro-lesson that makes Window Functions usable.

hook.text: Rank users by score without collapsing rows. Window functions compute across rows while keeping them all.

buildup.text: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY score DESC) assigns rank 1, 2, 3 to each row by score.

discovery.text: PARTITION BY splits the window: rank users within each country, not globally.

twist.text: Unlike GROUP BY, window functions don't collapse rows. Every original row stays in the result.

climax.text: Running totals, moving averages, and lag/lead comparisons — all powered by window functions.

punchline.text: Window functions: aggregate without collapsing. Powerful and elegant.

quiz.question: How do window functions differ from GROUP BY?
options: Window functions compute values across rows without collapsing them into groups
Window functions are faster in every case
GROUP BY can do everything window functions can
